flat_root_rules.guidance: Keep `dev/scripts/checks/` root reserved for public runnable entrypoints. New helper modules belong in an existing family directory or in a newly documented subpackage with a README.

namespace_family_rules.guidance: The `devctl` command check family is crowded. New flat `check_*` command modules should move into `dev/scripts/devctl/commands/check/` and leave only thin public wrappers at the root when a stable command entrypoint still needs one.
The `devctl` autonomy command family is crowded. Add new autonomy implementation under `dev/scripts/devctl/commands/autonomy/` instead of growing the flat command root.
The `devctl` docs-check command family is crowded. Keep root command files thin and move new docs implementation into `dev/scripts/devctl/commands/docs/`.
The review-channel command helpers should consolidate under `dev/scripts/devctl/commands/review_channel/` so root-level command modules stop accumulating bridge/event/support helpers.
Release command helpers belong under `dev/scripts/devctl/commands/release/` once the flat family is crowded.
Shipping/release command helpers should share the `dev/scripts/devctl/commands/release/` namespace instead of extending the flat command root.
Governance command helpers should consolidate under `dev/scripts/devctl/commands/governance/`.
Process command helpers should consolidate under `dev/scripts/devctl/commands/process/`.

directory_crowding_rules.guidance: Keep `dev/scripts/checks/` self-hosting: once the public check root is crowded, do not add more flat files there. Move implementation into focused subpackages and keep only stable wrappers at the root.

Keep `dev/scripts/devctl/` root reserved for stable public modules and compatibility seams. New implementation code belongs in topical subpackages such as `commands/`, `runtime/`, `review_channel/`, or later extracted platform layers.
The `devctl` command surface is already crowded. Add new command logic under topical command packages and keep root-level command modules from growing further.
Mirror `devctl` package topology in tests once the flat test root is crowded. New tests should land under topic-aligned test packages instead of the root test directory.

rules.guidance: Operator Console must consume stable runtime/snapshot contracts, not devctl command handlers, review-channel orchestrators, or raw guard entrypoints.
Review-channel backend code must not import UI/mobile surface layers; keep surfaces as clients of shared backend truth.

Portable runtime helpers must convert fixed-shape boundary data into typed contracts before field access. Replace `param: object` plus `getattr(param, 'field')` with a dataclass, TypedDict, or Protocol-backed seam.

allowed_public_shims.reason: Public guard entrypoints stay stable root scripts while implementation moves into package-owned modules.
Public review-probe entrypoints stay stable root scripts while implementation lives under `package_layout` or other focused helper packages.
Public report/runner entrypoints stay stable root scripts while helper logic moves into owned subpackages.

root_rules.guidance: Keep `dev/scripts/devctl/` compatibility seams auditable. Stable wrappers are allowed during staged module splits, but they should carry owner/reason/expiry/target metadata and should not accumulate indefinitely at the root.

rules.ai_instruction: Use `bridge` / `Review Bridge` as the canonical transport name. Retire legacy `code_audit` wording from code, docs, and generated surfaces so the bridge contract stays easy to follow.
Use `implementer` for the Claude-side role. Retire `coder` from review-channel code and maintainer docs so one role keeps one name.
